summary.description: Paramedic Checklist - All items must be checked before the start of each shift. Any outstanding items must be addressed within the response guidelines.

items.description: Number of ACRs/PCRs that are unfinished
items.notes: Each must be completed with 24 hours of call completion

items.description: Number of ACE reviews requiring comment
items.notes: Complete outstanding within 1 week of BH review
ACP Status is good if ACP

referenceGuide.ACRc: Ambulance Call Report completed — patient forms started at end of emergency call but not signed/completed. Paramedics have 24 hours to complete.
referenceGuide.ACEr: Ambulance Call Evaluation — peer/review team review of completed ACR where issues were found. Must reply within 1 week.
referenceGuide.CERT-DL: All paramedics in Ontario must maintain a valid class F Drivers License to operate an ambulance.
referenceGuide.CERT-Va: All paramedics must maintain vaccination and inoculation as prescribed by the Ontario Ministry of Health.
referenceGuide.CERT-CE: Indicates whether the medic is up to date on continuous education. All medics must continually upgrade.
referenceGuide.UNIF: Medics are allowed to order uniforms at set intervals. If damaged, they can request new shirts, shoes, pants, etc.
referenceGuide.CRIM: All medics are reviewed routinely through a criminal record check.
referenceGuide.ACP: If the medic has upped their certification level to ACP (Advanced Care Paramedic).
referenceGuide.VAC: Paramedics can request yearly vacation time and they require approval.
referenceGuide.MEALS: When services get very busy they occasionally miss a meal. These are meal claim forms outstanding for approval.
referenceGuide.OVER: Medics occasionally work overtime if on a call when shift ends. These are overtime claim forms awaiting approval.

criticalActions: Complete 2 overdue ACR entries before end of current shift — each must be done within 24 hours of call completion.
Update vaccination records (CERT-Va) — 1 outstanding vaccination needs to be brought up to date per Ontario Ministry of Health guidelines.
Follow up on 1 outstanding overtime request (OVER) — submit or check status of overtime claim.
